label: astronomic-metronomic-v1

scales.label: Earth to Mars
scales.bule: coding overhead (paths forked but not needed)

markdown:
# Astronomic Metronomic

**d_max = √(N·P / (ρ·c*²))**

How far can you whip a bit? Same formula at every scale.

| Scale | Distance | Fork N | Medium | Range | Reaches? |
|:------|:---------|:-------|:-------|:------|:---------|
| Across a table | 1 meter | 1 | Air | 3.2 m | yes |
| Across a room | 10 meters | 3 | Air + ambient noise | 5.5 m | no |
| Across a city | 10 km | 256 | Radio / cellular | 16.0 km | yes |
| Across the Earth | 12,742 km | 80 | Fiber optic / satellite | 282.8 km | no |
| Earth to Mars | 54.6 million km | 4,096 | Deep space (X-band) | 9.1 Gm | no |
| To Alpha Centauri | 4.24 light-years | 10⁹ | Interstellar void | 3.3 ly | no |
| Across the galaxy | 100,000 light-years | 10¹⁵ | Galactic medium | 334278822.4 ly | yes |

The child passing balls in §0 and the child juggling across the galaxy are the same formula.
The distance is in the taper. The message is in the balls.
